categories.name: TOGGLE_SETTING
categories.precondition: App is open and user can reach the settings area
abstract_steps: Open the app if not already in it
Navigate to the settings/preferences area via the overflow or 'more' entry
Tap the toggle if its state differs from desired
Verify the toggle visually reflects the new state
failure_modes: Tapping a toggle that is already in the desired state, flipping it the wrong way
Declaring completion before verifying the toggle changed
verification_checklist: Toggle indicator shows the desired on/off color/position

categories.name: MULTI_STEP_SETTINGS_THEN_RETURN
categories.precondition: Task requires changing one or more nested preferences and then returning to a specific starting screen
abstract_steps: Drill into a nested preferences subscreen if needed
Back-navigate out of the nested screen
Back-navigate out of settings until the originally requested screen is shown
failure_modes: Stopping completion signal while still inside settings or a nested subscreen
Skipping a needed back-press because intermediate screen looks like the target
Confusing the bottom-nav of the settings area with the main app navigation
verification_checklist: Final visible screen matches the requested destination's distinctive cues
All requested toggles ended in the requested state

categories.name: NAVIGATE_TO_TAB
categories.precondition: App has a primary tab/navigation bar and target tab is one of its entries
abstract_steps: Open the app if not already in it
Tap the entry matching the target tab name
Confirm the tab indicator/highlight moved to the target
failure_modes: Clicking a similarly named element outside the navigation bar
Assuming completion without checking the active-tab indicator
verification_checklist: Screen header/content matches the target tab
No login/sync prompt is mistaken as a failure state
